name: Khadija bint Hussain
title: Mourning Daughter
shortDescription: One of Imam Hussain's (AS) young daughters who was present in Karbala and was taken captive with the other children.
fullStory:
Khadija bint al-Hussain is one of the Master of Martyrs' (AS) daughters whose name is mentioned in some historical sources among the Karbala captives.

Named after her noble great-grandmother Lady Khadija al-Kubra (AS), she traveled to Karbala with her father's caravan during her childhood. On Ashura, like other children in the tents, she witnessed the horrific scenes of battle and severe thirst.

After her father's martyrdom, during the enemy's brutal attack on the tents, Khadija tasted the bitter flavor of orphanage, fear, and wandering in the desert. She was among the children who were taken towards Kufa and Damascus with bound hands on foot (or on unsaddled camels).

Enduring the sufferings of the long journey, seeing severed heads on spears, and the insults of Ibn Ziyad and Yazid's armies, made this innocent girl an eyewitness to the oppression of the Prophet's family.
quoteArabic: يا أَبَتاهُ! مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي أَيْتَمَنِي عَلَى صِغَرِ سِنِّي...
quote: (Condition of the young daughters:) O father! Who orphaned me at this young age...
